explanations.1: This is a shebang line that tells the system to run this file with the bash interpreter.
explanations.4: The 'local' keyword makes these variables only exist within this function, so they won't accidentally affect variables with the same names elsewhere.
explanations.10: The $((...)) syntax evaluates the math expression inside the parentheses and returns the result.
explanations.19: The '-ne' operator means 'not equal'; this checks if num2 is not zero before dividing.
explanations.32: $# is a special variable that counts how many arguments were passed to the script; this line checks if exactly 3 arguments were provided.
explanations.48: The double brackets [[ ]] allow pattern matching and are safer than single brackets in bash; here it's checking if the first argument is the word 'help'.
explanations.51: The '$@' expands to all arguments passed to the script, so you're forwarding them to the main function.
